commit.message:
Minimal repro: TempData.Get() does not consume value on first access

TempData.Get() calls _retainedKeys.Remove(key) before accessing the Data
property. Since TempData uses lazy loading, the Remove is a no-op when Get()
is the first access. The subsequent Load() triggered by accessing Data then
re-adds the key to _retainedKeys, so the value is never consumed and persists
across page refreshes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update sample-api submodule: full reset (baselines removed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: restart heartbeat thread after auto-update resume

When the auto-update false-positive path sets _shutdown to exit
the main loop, the heartbeat thread also exits. The resume path
was not restarting it, so lease renewals stopped and jobs would
fail with 'Coordinator rejected completion' after the lease
expired.

commit.message:
fix: add AMPLIHACK_PACKAGE_RECIPE_DIR env var to discovery (#3002) (#18)

* fix: add AMPLIHACK_PACKAGE_RECIPE_DIR env var to default search dirs (#3002)

The Rust recipe runner's `default_search_dirs()` did not include the
Python package's bundled recipe directory, causing a discovery mismatch
where Python could find recipes but the Rust binary could not.

Add support for the `AMPLIHACK_PACKAGE_RECIPE_DIR` environment variable
which is checked before other default paths. This allows:
- The Python wrapper (`run_recipe_via_rust`) to pass the package path
- Standalone callers to configure the package recipe location
- Both Python and Rust discovery to agree on available recipes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- empty AMPLIHACK_PACKAGE_RECIPE_DIR is ignored
- package recipe dir precedes RECIPE_RUNNER_RECIPE_DIRS entries

Adds coverage for the new discovery path introduced for issue #3002.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: parse tarpaulin coverage percentage correctly

tarpaulin reports both overall coverage and coverage delta in the same line:
  71.12% coverage, ... +0.15% change in coverage

The CI job was grabbing the last percentage token (0.15) instead of the
overall coverage (71.12), causing false failures on every PR.

Also includes the issue #3002 discovery env-var coverage tests.

commit.message:
perf: merge 4 integration test binaries into 1 (#8610)

## Summary

- Consolidates `integration_esbuild`, `integration_rolldown`,
`integration_rollup`, and `integration_test262` into a single
`integration` test binary via `tests/integration/main.rs`
- Eliminates 3 redundant ~80MB link steps on every incremental build
(~240MB saved)
- All 1704 tests continue to pass and can still be filtered (e.g. `cargo
test --test integration -- esbuild`)

## Details

The `tests/rolldown/` directory stays in place (it contains both Rust
modules and fixture data with intentionally broken syntax). The
integration binary references it via `#[path = "../rolldown/mod.rs"] mod
rolldown_tests;` to avoid both moving thousands of fixture files and
shadowing the external `rolldown` crate name.

A `dunce::canonicalize` call was added to
`IntegrationTestBuilder::new()` to resolve `..` path segments that
`file!()` produces when modules are loaded via `#[path]` attributes —
this fixes HMR watch file path matching.

## Measurements

### Local incremental build (touch `lib.rs`, rebuild tests)

| Metric | Old (4 binaries) | New (1 binary) | Savings |
|--------|-----------------|----------------|---------|
| Wall time | 4.47s | 3.73s | **-17%** |
| CPU time | 12.42s | 6.48s | **-48%** |
| Binaries linked | 4 (78+79+76+64 = 297MB) | 1 (82MB) | **-215MB disk**
|

### CI (ubuntu-latest)

| Step | Old (main, 4 binaries) | New (PR, 1 binary) | Savings |
|------|----------------------|-------------------|---------|
| Build | 51s | 47s | -4s |
| Run Test | 86s | 87s | ~same |
| Total | 137s | 134s | -3s |

CI savings are modest since the build step is cached. The main win is
**local incremental builds** — 48% less CPU work and 215MB less disk per
build cycle.

fix(openclaw): allow remote-node ingress to bypass cross-node SNAT drop

Cross-node traffic from envoy-internal pods gets SNATed to the node IP
by Cilium. Control plane node IPs get the kube-apiserver reserved identity,
which was being denied by the existing NetworkPolicy.

Add a CiliumNetworkPolicy to explicitly allow remote-node masqueraded
traffic on port 18790 (gateway-proxy).

commit.message:
feat: add RequireSubscriptionTier middleware + ErrNoRows sentinel

* scan: add ErrNoRows sentinel for errors.Is detection

scan.First previously returned errors.New("scan.First: no rows in result set"),
which callers could only check by comparing the error string. This adds a
package-level ErrNoRows sentinel so callers can use errors.Is(err, scan.ErrNoRows)
instead of fragile string matching.

First now wraps ErrNoRows with fmt.Errorf + %w, preserving the contextual prefix
while keeping the sentinel detectable. The test is updated accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(middleware,flags): RequireSubscriptionTier middleware + IsCustomFlagEnabled helper

- middleware.RequireSubscriptionTier(minTier) blocks users whose tier ranks
  below the required minimum (free<starter<pro<power<concierge); returns 401
  for unauthenticated, 403 for insufficient or unknown tier
- flags.IsCustomFlagEnabled(ctx, db, key, defaultValue) exposes queryFlag as
  a public API for arbitrary site_settings keys with caller-controlled default
- 4 new middleware tests + 2 new flags tests
